content.problemStatement:
The mayor of your city has decided to throw a party to gather the favour of his people in different regions of the city.

There are 333 distinct regions in the city namely AAA, BBB, CCC comprising of PAP_APA​, PBP_BPB​ and PCP_CPC​ number of people respectively.

However, the mayor knows that people of the region BBB are in conflict with people of regions AAA and CCC. So, there will be a conflict if people from region BBB are present at the party along with people from region AAA or CCC.

The mayor wants to invite as many people as possible and also avoid any conflicts. Help him invite **maximum** number of people to the party.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains three integers PAP_APA​, PBP_BPB​ and PCP_CPC​ - number of people living in regions AAA, BBB and CCC respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output the **maximum** number of people that can be invited to the party.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers PA, PB and PC — the number of people in regions AAA, BBB and CCC respectively.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the maximum number of people that can be invited without causing a conflict.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: PA+PC = 2+4 = 6, PB = 3 → max = 6.\nTest case 2: PA+PC = 1+2 = 3, PB = 5 → max = 5.\nTest case 3: PA+PC = 8+8 = 16, PB = 8 → max = 16.
First case: PA+PC = 1000+1 = 1001, PB = 1000 → max = 1001.\nSecond case: PA+PC = 7+2 = 9, PB = 9 → both equal, max = 9.

solution.approach: For each test case there are only two conflict‑free possibilities: invite everyone from region BBB (PB people) or invite everyone from regions AAA and CCC together (PA + PC people). Any subset of AAA or CCC alone would be smaller than PA + PC because all numbers are positive. Therefore the answer is simply max(PB, PA + PC). The algorithm iterates over the test cases, computes this maximum and prints it.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        PA = int(next(it))
        PB = int(next(it))
        PC = int(next(it))
        out_lines.append(str(max(PB, PA + PC)))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
